content:
# Titus Chapter 3 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (Aleph, 01), page 223 | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/TIT_003_page_001.jpg

1. Remind them to be subject to rulers, to authorities, to be obedient, to be ready for every good work,

2. to blaspheme no one, to be non-contentious, gentle, showing all meekness toward all men.

3. For we also were once foolish, disobedient, being led astray, serving various desires and pleasures, living in malice and envy, hateful, hating one another.

4. But when the kindness and the love-for-mankind of the Savior of us, God, appeared,

5. not from works which are in righteousness which we did, but according to the mercy of him, he saved us, through [the] washing of regeneration and renewal of [the] Holy Spirit,

6. which he poured out upon us richly through Yeshua Christos the Savior of us,

7. that having been justified by the grace of that [one], we might become heirs according to [the] hope of eternal life.

8. Faithful [is] the word, and concerning these things I want you to affirm strongly, that the [ones] having believed God may be careful to stand before [others] in good works. These things are good and profitable to men.

9. But foolish questionings and genealogies and strife and contentions about [the] law, avoid; for they are unprofitable and vain.

10. A heretical man after a first and second admonition reject,

11. knowing that such a one has been perverted and sins, being self-condemned.

12. When I send Artemas to you, or Tychikos, be diligent to come to me at Nikopolis, for I have decided to winter there.

13. Zenas the lawyer and Apollos diligently send forward, that nothing may be lacking to them.

14. And let also ours learn to stand before [others] in good works for the necessary needs, that they may not be unfruitful.

15. All the [ones] with me greet you. Greet the [ones] loving us in faith. The grace [be] with all of you.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 1** — Sinaiticus reads ΥΠΟΜΙΜΝΗΣΚΕ ΑΥΤΟΥΣ ΑΡΧΑΙΣ ΕΞΟΥΣΙΑΙΣ ΥΠΟΤΑΣΣΕΣΘΑΙ ΠΕΙΘΑΡΧΕΙΝ, placing "to be subject" (ΥΠΟΤΑΣΣΕΣΘΑΙ) before "to be obedient" (ΠΕΙΘΑΡΧΕΙΝ). The word order is maintained literally in translation.

2. **Verse 4** — Sinaiticus reads Η ΧΡΗΣΤΟΤΗΣ ΚΑΙ Η ΦΙΛΑΝΘΡΩΠΙΑ, "the kindness and the love-for-mankind." ΦΙΛΑΝΘΡΩΠΙΑ is rendered hyper-literally as "love-for-mankind" to preserve the compound Greek word (ΦΙΛΟΣ + ΑΝΘΡΩΠΟΣ).

3. **Verse 5** — Sinaiticus reads ΟΥΚ ΕΞ ΕΡΓΩΝ ΤΩΝ ΕΝ ΔΙΚΑΙΟΣΥΝΗ, "not from works which are in righteousness," which differs from the Byzantine text's ΕΞ ΕΡΓΩΝ ΤΩΝ ΕΝ ΔΙΚΑΙΟΣΥΝΗ Α ("from works of righteousness which"). The Sinaiticus phrasing is preserved literally.

4. **Verse 5** — ΠΑΛΙΓΓΕΝΕΣΙΑΣ (regeneration/new-birth) is a rare word appearing only here and in Matthew 19:28 in the New Testament. Rendered "regeneration" to maintain the literal sense of ΠΑΛΙΝ (again) + ΓΕΝΕΣΙΣ (birth/origin).

5. **Verse 8** — Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΟΙΣΤΑΣΘΑΙ ΚΑΛΩΝ ΕΡΓΩΝ, "to stand before in good works." ΠΡΟΙΣΤΗΜΙ carries the dual sense of "to stand before / preside over" and "to devote oneself to / maintain." The literal "stand before" is preserved.

6. **Verse 10** — ΑΙΡΕΤΙΚΟΝ (heretical) — this is the only New Testament occurrence of this word. It refers to one who causes division by factious choice (from ΑΙΡΕΣΙΣ, "choosing/faction"). Rendered "heretical" as the most literal English cognate.

7. **Verse 12** — Sinaiticus reads ΑΡΤΕΜΑΝ (Artemas), not the variant ΑΡΤΕΜΑΝ found in some witnesses. ΤΥΧΙΚΟΝ (Tychikos) is transliterated from the Greek rather than Latinized to "Tychicus."

8. **Verse 14** — Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΟΙΣΤΑΣΘΑΙ ΚΑΛΩΝ ΕΡΓΩΝ ΕΙΣ ΤΑΣ ΑΝΑΓΚΑΙΑΣ ΧΡΕΙΑΣ, "to stand before in good works for the necessary needs." This echoes the same verb ΠΡΟΙΣΤΗΜΙ from verse 8.

9. **Verse 15** — Sinaiticus reads Η ΧΑΡΙΣ ΜΕΤΑ ΠΑΝΤΩΝ ΥΜΩΝ, "The grace with all of you," without the addition of ΑΜΗΝ ("Amen") found in later manuscripts. The shorter ending is preserved as read from the manuscript.

10. **General note** — Titus 3:1 begins at the transition from column 1 to column 2 on page 223. The chapter concludes in column 3 with the subscript ΠΡΟΣ ΤΙΤΟΝ before Philemon begins in column 4.

-- EMET
